title: What is OVOSEC and how does it secure my connection?
subtitle: An overview of OVOSEC secure utility and encrypted transit.
content: OVOSEC is a next-generation encrypted relay utility designed to optimize global network latency and protect data integrity. Utilizing an open-source high-performance core architecture, OVOSEC channels your data streams through enterprise-grade encrypted transit nodes, masking your transmission traces and accelerating cross-border productivity workflows seamlessly.

title: How do I set up the OVOSEC utility on my device?
subtitle: Step-by-step instructions for installation and setup.
content:
To set up the OVOSEC utility on your device, follow these steps:

  1. Complete your plan selection on our official website (ovosec.com).
  2. Navigate to the Download page and acquire the secure application package tailored for your operating system (Windows, macOS, Android, or iOS).
  3. Launch the application, copy your unique Access Configuration URL from your Client Dashboard, and click "Import Configuration" within the app interface to instantly synchronize your authorized relay endpoints.

title: What is the core architecture of OVOSEC?
subtitle: An overview of OVOSEC security utility and encrypted transit.
content: OVOSEC is a next-generation encrypted relay utility designed to optimize global network latency and protect data integrity. By channeling your data streams through enterprise-grade encrypted transit nodes, OVOSEC masks your transmission traces and accelerating cross-border productivity workflows seamlessly.

title: Which operating systems are compatible with OVOSEC?
subtitle: Checking device compatibility and operating system requirements.
content: OVOSEC is fully compatible with Windows, macOS, iOS, Android, and Linux platforms. You can acquire the secure application package tailored for your operating system on our official Download page.

title: Why does the accelerated connection automatically disconnect when my phone goes to sleep?
subtitle: Preventing mobile operating systems from sleeping OVOSEC.
content:
Aggressive mobile operating system battery savers often terminate high-performance background daemons. To prevent this, please apply the following adjustments:

  • Android: Go to your phone's Settings -> Apps -> OVOSEC -> Battery -> Set to "Unrestricted" or "No Optimization".
  • iOS: Ensure that "Background App Refresh" is enabled for OVOSEC under iOS Settings -> OVOSEC.

title: None of the above steps resolved my issue. How do I contact official engineering support?
subtitle: Submitting diagnostic tickets to our technical team.
content:
We are committed to your seamless connectivity. If you encounter an unresolved routing conflict, please submit a diagnostic ticket to our technical queue at support@ovosec.com.

To help us resolve it within 24 hours, please include:

  • Your registered account email.
  • Your current operating system version (e.g., Windows 11, iOS 17).
  • A brief description or screenshot of the application log/error status.

title: Why is my profile list empty, or why does it say "No Profile Found" after importing my URL?
subtitle: Triggering initial synchronization with the billing server.
content:
This can occur if the application fails to perform its initial handshake with our billing servers due to local network jitter. To force a sync, please follow these steps:

  1. In the app, go to the Profiles tab and tap the three dots (menu) next to your imported OVOSEC link.
  2. Click "Force Update" or "Reload Profile".
  3. Go back to the main screen and click the central "Connect" button. This will instantly fetch and populate all authorized low-latency secure relay paths.

title: Why did all my secure relay paths suddenly stop working after a long period of inactivity?
subtitle: Resolving protocol version mismatches after long inactivity.
content:
To maintain military-grade security encryption, our backend infrastructure periodically upgrades its underlying transmission protocols. If your client version is heavily outdated, it will fail to handshake with the upgraded nodes.

Solution: Please visit our official Download page to fetch and install the latest stable version of the OVOSEC utility for your device. After updating the software, re-sync your Access Configuration URL to restore instantaneous low-latency connectivity.

title: Why can't I open the Wi-Fi login/authentication page at hotels, airports, or universities?
subtitle: Bypassing unencrypted captive portal interception.
content:
Public networks utilize an interception technique called "Captive Portals" to force users onto a login screen. Because OVOSEC's active security core blocks unauthorized local tracking and untrusted network redirections, it may prevent this unencrypted login page from opening.

Solution: Temporarily turn OFF the OVOSEC connection or disable the boot-up launch feature. Open your web browser, complete the hotel/campus network authentication first, verify that you have basic internet access, and then toggle OVOSEC back ON to secure your session.
